subject: Re: unbinned k3 tomograms on RTX3090
body:
Hi Dan,

Glad you got a bit further, keep fighting the good fight!! Nothing immediately comes to mind, can you reconstruct at a smaller pixel size from the same data? (Tilt series in warp still unbinned, reconstructing particles at a significantly larger pixel size) This might help to rule out the ‘aliasing free box size’ type issues

Good luck!

Alister

> On 26 Mar 2021, at 15:09, Daniel Mann <d.mann@fz-juelich.de> wrote:
> 
> Dear Alister and all,
> 
> using only one GPU indeed helped me to cross the barrier for CTF estimation. Reconstruction and template matching worked fine as well, thanks a lot :)
> 
> However, subtomo reconstruction with 256px boxes produces completely black subtomos.
> 
> From the mail archive:
> 
> - I'm using english layout ("." separator instead of ",")
> 
> - I don't have phase shift (no phase plate)
> 
> - no error message that GPU runs out of memory. Actually tried setting 64px boxes with same px size and 64px boxes with binned pixel size; still black.
> 
> Any ideas?
> 
> Cheers,
> 
> Dan
> 
> Also tried skipping normalisation, no success.
> 
> Am 11.03.2021 um 09:52 schrieb Alister Burt:
>> Hi Dan,
>> 
>> Your workstation sounds like a beast!!
>> 
>> I’m not sure it will help in your case but I had luck using only 1 GPU to halve RAM requirements in M (Dimitry said multiple GPUs share a subset of your RAM).
>> 
>> Depending on resolution/number of particles per field of view you might have a really hard time getting this to run in M at all even if CTF estimation works, might be worth stepping down the pixel size a bit less? Nyquist is always exciting!
>> 
>> There have also been quite a few problems reported with 3090 cards and Warp which might not be helping your situation (Tom Dendooven and Teige Matthews-Palmer have been on the list about it, maybe others I’m forgetting too)
>> 
>> Cheers and good luck!
>> 
>> Alister
>> 
>> 
>> 
>>> On 11 Mar 2021, at 08:29, Daniel Mann <d.mann@fz-juelich.de> wrote:
>>> 
>>> ﻿Hi all,
>>> 
>>> after bin1 data from K3 tomograms refined to Nyquist in Warp/M I tried
>>> to repeat the process for unbinned K3 superres data (11k x 8k) on 2x
>>> RTX3090 GPUs (24GB each, 64 CPUs, 256GB RAM).
>>> 
>>> Warp crashes at CTF estimation after IMOD alignment with
>>> 
>>> System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to read or write protected
>>> memory. This is often an indication that other memory is corrupt.
>>> 
>>> Which I interpret as an out-of-memory message, although I don't really
>>> see a strong GPU memory peak in Task Manager.
>>> 
>>> If my interpretation is correct, is there any strategy to reduce GPU
>>> memory usage, e.g. CTF window size, range or other cmd tweaks? I'm not
>>> using any denoising or so and use 1 progress per GPU in the global settings.
>>> 
>>> Thanks for your help!
>>> 
>>> Best wishes from Jülich
>>> 
>>> Dan
>>> 
>>> 
>>>
